score_reasoning: North American Banking Company demonstrates moderate-to-strong buying signals across multiple dimensions. Primary strengths: (1) Multi-location operational complexity (6 branches) with lean IT department creates clear pain point for AIS solutions; (2) Recent Apiture platform investments and FedNow/RTP real-time payments expansion (July 2025) demonstrate active technology modernization and willingness to invest in operational improvements; (3) High-volume ACH ODFI operations (#28 nationally) requiring sophisticated process automation and control systems; (4) Recent leadership promotions in Payments & Technology group signal capability-building and expansion mode; (5) Federal Reserve member bank facing June 19, 2026 Nacha Phase II compliance deadline creates urgency for operations automation and monitoring system enhancements. Timing is favorable for discussing multi-location process standardization, ACH operations automation, compliance system improvements, and IT infrastructure optimization. Approach should leverage recent technology investments as proof of innovation momentum and position AIS as scalable partner to support growth without large in-house engineering team investment. Key decision-makers are relationship-driven and community-focused—emphasize local partnership and accountability.
next_action: Qualify Michael Bilski or Brad Huckle on multi-location operations automation and ACH compliance system opportunities. Reference recent Apiture investment, real-time payments platform expansion, and June 2026 Nacha Phase II deadline. Discuss how AIS can support scaling operations across 6 locations and ACH infrastructure modernization without requiring in-house engineering team expansion. Schedule initial call emphasizing local partnership and long-term support.
notes: Company Details: Founded July 21, 1998 by co-founders Michael Bilski and Brad Huckle (both formerly at Princeton Bank). Co-founders raised $4 million from ~100 shareholders (many employees) and launched bank with 10 initial employees. Currently $1.2B-$1.4B in total assets (Q1 2025: $1.37B), 95 employees as of Sept 2025, 6 locations. Privately held, Member FDIC (#34836). One of Minnesota's strongest community banks with B+ health rating. | Locations: Roseville HQ (2230 Albert St N), Woodbury, Minneapolis (4999 France Ave S Suite 120), Hastings (1620 S Frontage Rd), Shoreview (435 Highway 96 West), Maple Grove (11821 Arbor Lakes Parkway, opened late 2022). | Tech Stack: Apiture Digital Banking Platform (consumer/business banking, real-time payments via FedNow and RTP). Office 365 mentioned in tech stack. Member of Nacha, UMACHA, BankIn Minnesota (formerly ICBM), Shazam, US Faster Payments Council. | Recent Activity & Achievements: (1) First Apiture client to enable real-time payments via both FedNow Service and RTP (Clearing House) platforms simultaneously (July 2025); (2) FedNow early adopter—one of 57 certified organizations at FedNow launch (July 19, 2023); (3) RTP (Real Time Payments) participant for over 1 year prior to FedNow; (4) Michael Bilski serves as Chairman of US Faster Payments Council; (5) Paul Tomasofsky appointed to second term (2024) on Nacha's Risk Management Advisory Group (RMAG); (6) 28th largest ACH ODFI nationally by transaction volume (2024); 23rd largest in 2022; (7) CEO Michael Bilski actively speaking at Nacha Payments Innovation Alliance on Risk Assessments for Instant Payments and ACH; (8) Employee promotions signal expansion: Alex Bilski promoted to CCCP (Certified Compliance and Control Professional), Nora Lovaas to AAP/NCP, Justin Adducci to AVP Business Banking. | Pain Signals & Opportunities: (1) Multi-branch complexity: 6 locations require unified systems management, standardized processes, and branch automation potential; (2) ACH compliance urgency: June 19, 2026 Nacha Enhanced Fraud Monitoring Rule Phase II deadline approaching—requires enhanced risk-based ACH monitoring capabilities; (3) High-volume payment processing: #28 ACH ODFI nationally requires sophisticated operational infrastructure, control systems, and compliance automation; (4) Technology modernization in progress: Recent Apiture investments and real-time payments platform expansion demonstrate active modernization and willingness to invest; (5) Lean IT structure: Small IT department (Dan Ley, AVP IT) appears to be single point of contact, limiting capacity for complex infrastructure projects; (6) Growth mode: Leadership promotions, multi-branch expansion, active hiring (Universal Banker, Loan Operations Specialist roles) all signal scaling/expansion; (7) Payment processing integration: Expanding from ACH/RTP/FedNow requires ongoing system integration, operations optimization, and control enhancements. | Decision-Maker Profiles: Michael Bilski (CEO/Chairman, founder)—fourth-generation banker, 25+ years banking experience, thought leader in payments innovation (US Faster Payments Council), mission-driven on modernization and community banking; relationship-focused, prefers personal engagement with trusted partners. Brad Huckle (President/Chief Lending Officer, co-founder)—25+ years banking experience, business/relationship-driven, focuses on small business market support; accessible, collaborative decision-maker. Katie Stockman (CFO)—likely budget authority for capital infrastructure investments. Dan Ley (AVP IT)—key technical decision-maker for systems/infrastructure projects, probable liaison for automation initiatives. Paul Tomasofsky (SVP Merchant Payments) & Alex Bilski (AVP Payments & Technology)—key for ACH operations and compliance system discussions. | Differentiation Opportunities for AIS: (1) ACH compliance automation—help NABC implement enhanced risk-based ACH monitoring for June 2026 Nacha Phase II deadline; (2) Multi-branch process standardization and automation; (3) Real-time payments infrastructure support and integration optimization; (4) Building automation and facility controls across 6 locations; (5) Operational scaling partnership to support continued growth without in-house engineering team expansion; (6) Control systems integration for payments processing infrastructure. | Outreach Strategy: Lead with Nacha Phase II compliance deadline (June 19, 2026) and recent real-time payments platform expansion as hooks. Emphasize AIS's local Minnesota presence, accountability, and full-service capability (engineering + fabrication + installation + support). Position as scalable partner for growing bank managing multi-location complexity. Reference Apiture Innovator Award and FedNow/RTP adoption as proof of their innovation momentum. Suggest conversation with Michael Bilski or Brad Huckle on how to support scaling without large in-house engineering investment. | Research Notes: Michael Bilski LinkedIn profile shows active engagement on payments topics (Nacha speaking roles, FedNow updates, industry thought leadership). Brad Huckle provides direct phone/email contact on LinkedIn. Company actively participates in Minnesota banking ecosystem (Community Impact Month, Nacha memberships, SBA partnerships). Employee-owned culture and long-standing employee tenure suggest relationship-driven decision-making. No visible resistance to external partnerships or outsourced expertise.
